mnemonics: NOT AND is NAND, NOT OR is NOR. Universal gates, one is all you need for the door.
keyPoints: NAND and NOR gates are 'universal', meaning any Boolean function can be implemented using only that one type of gate.
A NAND gate is an AND gate followed by a NOT gate. Its output is LOW only when all inputs are HIGH.
A NOR gate is an OR gate followed by a NOT gate. Its output is HIGH only when all inputs are LOW.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to invert the output when building a basic gate (like AND or OR) from universal gates.
Assuming NAND and NOR gates behave like AND and OR gates because their symbols look similar, ignoring the bubble which signifies inversion.
